metadata.notes: agent=no — agy has NO in-REPL clear/new/reset command (palette enumerated live; /clear returns 'No matches').

assessment.body:
## Verdict

**agent=no.** Session-reset requires an in-REPL command that ends the current conversation and starts a fresh one with a new session_id (claudecode /clear, gemini-cli /clear|/new). `agy` has NO such command. I enumerated the full live slash-command palette (38 commands) by scrolling the picker under tmux: there is NO `/clear`, `/new`, or `/reset`. Typing `/clear` into the palette returns 'No matches'. The closest commands are `/rename` (renames the current conversation in place — same session_id) and `/btw` (side question without interrupting) — neither rotates the session_id. Starting a new conversation requires relaunching the binary (a restart, scenario 1.4-adjacent), not an in-session reset. There is no top-level reset flag in `agy --help` either.

**daemon=n/a.** With no agent surface to trigger the reset, there is no transcript signal to observe; the pillar is n/a (no feature → frozen).

**driver=ready** is moot for a frozen cell — the reset_session step type exists in the scaffold (stubbed) but there is no agent command for it to drive.
caveats: Re-assess if a future agy release adds a /clear|/new|/reset REPL command — the daemon would then observe a conv-id rotation (a new <conv-id> dir under the brain store) exactly like the gemini-cli session-reset cell.
SYSTEM/CHECKPOINT lines in the transcript are auto-generated planner checkpoints, NOT user-triggered resets; the parser skips them.

sources.note: enumerated 38 commands; no /clear, /new, /reset; /clear filter → 'No matches'. Closest: /rename (in-place, same session_id)
no top-level reset flag (only --continue/--conversation for resume)

recipe.notes: agent_supports=no — agy has no in-REPL clear/new/reset command (live palette enumeration; /clear → 'No matches'). No recipe; this cell is frozen.
